title: Eriosyce litoralis
taxonomy: Eriosyce litoralis belongs to the family Cactaceae, which comprises all types of cacti. It is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. Within this tribe, Eriosyce litoralis is placed in the subtribe Cacteinae. This specific classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other cacti species. Eriosyce litoralis is also commonly referred to as the "Coquimbo cactus" in some sources.

preparations: Eriosyce litoralis, also known as the coastal pitaya or Chilean pitaya, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its various health benefits. intro: Eriosyce litoralis, also known as the Coastal Eriosyce, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae, specifically within the subfamily Opuntioideae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wound healing, pain relief, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriosyce litoralis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriosyce litoralis is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and ointments to treat skin irritations, wounds, and pain, as well as to support the immune system.
